steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence lists three parallel destinations as items in a series: Wellington, Walgett, and Moree. Each item contains a dependent clause introduced with “where,” which means a comma appears within each item.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule for complex series
steps.content:
A semicolon replaces a comma to separate items in a series when those items contain internal punctuation.

Option B correctly creates “cinema; and to Moree,” which produces the proper structure: “drove to Wellington, where they interviewed locals; to Walgett, where they picketed a segregated cinema; and to Moree, where they staged a protest at a segregated public pool.” Options A, C, and D either use commas where semicolons are needed, place a semicolon incorrectly, or employ a colon improperly.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
